text:
1. Land NMP #2809: amend ADR-0075 so “diagnostic surface” is explicitly allowed while app/native surfaces still cannot expose Trellis types.
  2. Add an NMP-owned X-Ray projection or nmp-devtools stream. It should expose ordered open/replace/refresh/close events, scope/projection owner, reason/provenance, transaction/
     revision, shared-owner/refcount state, and teardown cascade. [...]   5. For Chirp, expose this as a dev-build-only declared projection or devtools channel, then Chirp can render the pane. Chirp should not parse Trellis keys or receive ResourcePlan,
     ResourceKey, Graph, etc. [...] - #2809 ADR-0075 amendment: diagnostic surface is not app surface — propose nmp-devtools — the core proposal for the X-Ray/devtools diagnostic surface; open, `status:needs-adr`
- #2695, #2690 — unrelated (matched only generic "epic"/"tracking" terms)

No dedicated epic for X-Ray/devtools exists. The closest ancestor epics, **#2626** ("Epic: adopt Trellis as NMP's private reconciliation substrate") and **#2627** ("ADR: Trellis is a private reconciliation substrate for NMP sessions"), are both **CLOSED** — that groundwork is done; #2809 is the unepic'd follow-on.

**pablof7z/chirp**
- #30 X-Ray Feed: developer overlay showing live relay-subscription reconciliation with causes — the Chirp-side diagnostics pane; open, no epic label
- #2 [Epic] Complete NMP migration update and architecture validation for Chirp — existing epic but general migration/validation, not diagnostics-specific
- #9 Validate Chirp showcase product smoke coverage after NMP migration — adjacent QA, not diagnostics

## Epic/tracking issue check
**No epic currently ties this workstream together.** Nothing tracks "NMP X-Ray diagnostic surface / nmp-devtools + Trellis trace tooling + Chirp diagnostics pane" as one initiative. #2809, #114 (closed), and #30 cross-reference each other explicitly but aren't rolled up under a tracking issue. Nearest analogues: trellis #87 (showcase/Observatory epic — adjacent but scoped to demo apps) and NMP's now-closed #2626/#2627 (the reconciliation-substrate epic that preceded this).

## Full summaries

**NMP#2809** — OPEN. Labels: priority:p2, category:decision, status:needs-adr, area:architecture. Proposes amending ADR-0075 to split "app surface" (unchanged: no Trellis vocabulary crosses UniFFI/web) from a new "diagnostic surface": a dev-build-only `nmp-devtools` crate exposing Trellis trace/audit data via an MCP server (`why_subscription_open`, `why_view_stale`, `what_closed_this_relay`, `list_scope_inventory`, `replay_session`) plus redacted trace export for bug reports. Explicitly excludes metrics/dashboards/log aggregation — receipts only. Names four upstream trellis prerequisites: #114 (serializable traces), #125 (audit retention toggle), #122 (typed resource keys), #126 (scope reclamation) — **all four now closed**. Sequenced after NMP #2808 (shadow→authority promotion), which is also now closed/merged (PR #2832, "Promote Trellis feed-session reconciliation to authority," merged today, 2026-07-03). Every stated prerequisite is done; #2809 is unblocked, just needs the ADR decision. No comments. [...] Assistant: **Epic created: [nostr-multi-platform#2858](https://github.com/pablof7z/nostr-multi-platform/issues/2858)** — no epic existed before (the surveyor confirmed nothing ties this workstream together; the closest ancestors, NMP #2626/#2627, are closed). I cross-linked it from chirp#30 and NMP#2809, and yes: **time travel is in the plan as committed scope (Phase E), not a stretch goal** — per your message, it's written up as the recorded/replayable debugging capsule with scrubbing, not log viewing. [...] **The plan (five phases in the epic):**

- **Phase A — ADR-0075 amendment** (#2809): define "diagnostic surface" vs "app surface," and update doctrine-lint plus the Trellis public-API leak ratchet (#2642) so the exemption is enforced mechanically, not case-by-case.
- **Phase B — nmp-devtools stream**: ordered open/replace/refresh/close events with owner/provenance/revision/refcount/teardown data; join Trellis `ResourcePlan` → `ApplyDependentInterestDelta` outcomes into bounded retained records (today they're test-only traces); correlate with the existing relay diagnostics; and define the **NMP-owned receipt shape** so Chirp never sees `ResourcePlan`/`ResourceKey`/`Graph`.
- **Phase C — agent-first prover (your CLI/REPL/TUI ask)**: the MCP server (`why_subscription_open`, `why_view_stale`, `what_closed_this_relay`, `list_scope_inventory`, `replay_session`) plus a headless CLI that drives a real Chirp session against real relays and also loads/scrubs exported capsules. Exit criterion baked into the epic: *an agent can answer "why is my feed empty?" from receipts alone.* This lands **before** the pane, so agents can iterate on the diagnostic itself.
- **Phase D — Chirp X-Ray pane** (chirp#30 v1): dev-build-only declared projection/devtools channel; Chirp renders, never parses.
- **Phase E — time travel** (chirp#30 v2): dev-only recorder, ring-buffer retention with explicit export, redaction policy with local/debug vs shareable modes, correlation persisted into the capsule so scrubbing reconstructs full cause chains (refcount 2→1 vs 1→0→CLOSE), and replay-from-capsule-alone so a redacted trace attached to an issue is debuggable without the device or live relays. [...] 
- **Phase A — ADR-0075 amendment** (#2809): define "diagnostic surface" vs "app surface," and update doctrine-lint plus the Trellis public-API leak ratchet (#2642) so the exemption is enforced mechanically, not case-by-case.
- **Phase B — nmp-devtools stream**: ordered open/replace/refresh/close events with owner/provenance/revision/refcount/teardown data; join Trellis `ResourcePlan` → `ApplyDependentInterestDelta` outcomes into bounded retained records (today they're test-only traces); correlate with the existing relay diagnostics; and define the **NMP-owned receipt shape** so Chirp never sees `ResourcePlan`/`ResourceKey`/`Graph`. [...] **5. [Should-fix — Phase A] "Compiled out of release builds" needs a mechanical definition and CI enforcement.** `cfg(debug_assertions)` is the wrong tool (release-with-debug builds exist; per-platform build configs differ). Suggested wording: nmp-devtools is a separate crate behind an off-by-default cargo feature; CI asserts each platform's release artifact dependency graph (`cargo tree`) and/or symbol table contains no nmp-devtools; the ratchet rule is dependency-graph-based — an allowlist file of crates permitted to depend on `trellis-*` (existing private adapters + nmp-devtools), where adding a line is the ratchet violation. [...] - **PR #2859** — the ADR amendment itself, closing #2809, with the lint classifier scoped to `crates/nmp-devtools/src` and a regression test. **Phase A: done.**
- **PRs #2860/#2861** — `XrayReceipt` + bounded ordered `XrayReceiptStream` + the Trellis→receipt adapter. Most of Phase B.
- **PR #2862** — `XrayCapsule` envelope (schema versions, producer metadata, redaction mode, **symbolication manifest**), stable shareable pseudonymization, and `XrayProbe` helpers for all five debug queries. That's the Fable-review findings implemented — whoever built it worked straight from the updated epic.

And the transport was decided by implementation exactly as I'd have recommended: `nmp-devtools` is a **sidecar with no runtime/app dependents**; receipts never touch the projection pipeline. The only scenario where you'd need to weigh in is if you *disagree* with the sidecar shape — speak now, before Phase D wires Chirp to it.
